claim: For all integers n > 2, Euler's totient phi(n) is even.
predicate: n > 2 and phi(n) % 2 == 0

claim: For all integers n > 2, if n is a power of 2, then phi(n) is divisible by 4.
predicate: n > 2 and (n & (n-1)) == 0 and phi(n) % 4 == 0

claim: For all integers n > 2, if n is an odd prime, then phi(n) is divisible by 2 but not by 4.
predicate: n > 2 and is_prime(n) and n % 2 == 1 and phi(n) % 2 == 0 and phi(n) % 4 != 0

claim: For all integers n > 2, if n is the product of two distinct odd primes, then phi(n) is divisible by 4.
